result:
Done — the experiment is pre-registered in `runs/pending_experiment.json`, figcheck passes, and the hinge code in the brief is verified to run with the correct grid geometry. The harness will take it from here.

## What this iteration proposes (exp 34)

**Hardest-impostor margin loss** (category: loss — a family absent from all 8 plateau experiments). One change, entirely inside `model/model.py`'s `loss_fn`: alongside the unchanged Gaussian-bump CE + coord L2 + conf BCE, each training crop gets a hinge on the same C4-voted logits the decode uses — the best-scoring cell **outside a 7×7 ring** around the truth must trail the best logit in the **3×3 truth block** by ≥ 1.0 logit (λ = 0.5). Forward pass, ONNX graph, sampler, schedule, and calibration are all untouched, so deployment gates and the operating point are identical to the champion's by construction.

**Why this, given the pivot directive.** The iter-12 probes pinpoint the bottleneck all eight reverts jointly point at: ranking quality — the true cell makes the field's top-20 up to 58% of the time but is top-1 on only 2%, in *every* bucket including midday (which also rules out learned relighting as the binding lever). The last 8 changed what feeds the ranking (capacity, readout tap, dispatcher, learned map) or how it's thresholded (calibration ×2), but the signal that *creates* the ranking has been pure likelihood since exp 2: smeared CE never concentrates gradient on the specific distant lookalike that outranks the truth. Place-recognition systems (NetVLAD/FaceNet lineage) train exactly this failure with hardest-negative margins.

**Pre-registered prediction:** worst-case median 797.91 → 600–750 m, improving across all 24 cells at comparable coverage; probe-level, berlin-midday top-1 2% → ≥8% and peak mass 0.14 → ≥0.25. Falsification fork: if ranking improves but kept medians don't, the wall is descriptor information content — redirecting future work to the trunk, not the loss.

**Side finding worth knowing:** exp 33 (sharpness jitter) was **never scored** — munich training was OOM-killed (`loop.sh line 203: Killed`, `score_s: 0`, `areas: []`), the third infrastructure death recorded as a 1e9. I saved this to memory (`exp33-oom-never-scored`) so future iterations don't treat it as a refutation, and noted why I still pivoted rather than rerunning it: both prior reruns of infra-killed experiments (24, 28) failed on the metric, and blur-jitter mainly hardens against crisper-than-eval inputs the frozen eval set barely contains.

tool_input.command: for a in frankfurt munich prignitz; do echo "== $a"; tail -3 runs/20260722_073412_iter6/train_$a.log; done; echo; grep -n "score\|Traceback\|Error" loop_console.log | tail -20
tool_input.description: Check other area logs and loop console for scoring failure

tool_input.command: sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" | python3 -m json.tool
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_svg FROM experiments WHERE id=33;" > /tmp/exp33.svg; wc -c /tmp/exp33.svg; head -c 4000 /tmp/exp33.svg
